title: The Memory Wall
topic: Why Modern Computing Is Bottlenecked by Data, Not Compute
summary: A first-principles, chalkboard-styled tour of why 2026 computing is bottlenecked by data movement, not arithmetic: chiplet CPUs and 3D stacking, the HBM/DDR5/HBF memory hierarchy, why LLM decode is memory-bound, why interconnects (PCIe/NVLink/UCIe) are the new bus, the four fixes architects are pursuing, and what it means for how software engineers should write and profile code.

concepts.title: The Modern CPU Isn't What You Think
The Memory Hierarchy Is the Real CPU
The Four Fixes Architects Are Pursuing
What This Means for Software Engineers

images.prompt: A moody, cinematic photo-illustration of a dark green-grey chalkboard densely covered in hand-drawn chip diagrams: a chiplet package, a memory-hierarchy pyramid, and arrows of data flowing between a small CPU sketch and a tall stack of memory blocks. A single warm lamp casts light from the upper left; chalk dust drifts visibly in the beam. Soft pastel chalk colors (dusty pink, sky blue, sage green, warm gold) for all the linework against the dark slate; warm, patient, academic mood; no legible words baked in, only scribbly diagram shapes and arrows. ~1200x800px, 3:2 landscape; compose with the richest diagram detail centered and safe margins on all sides, since it is letterboxed into a 3:2 box.
images.alt: A dark chalkboard densely covered in hand-drawn chip and memory-hierarchy diagrams, lit by a single warm lamp
images.caption: Every diagram in this book started life exactly like this one: chalk on slate.
